annotation_basis: Blind annotation from source O&M text only.

concept_ground_truth.label: T1
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: At Velorian PackLine-287 AWD, reproduce the service-disconnect seating complaint by noting latch feel, insertion force, handle flushness, any HVIL warning, and whether the orange handle sits proud of the battery access opening or snaps back after seating. Keep the opening complaint tied to the actual handle position and HVIL response so later seat checks stay on the same disconnect boundary.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T1 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T2
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Depower the pack, verify zero residual high-voltage potential at the service-disconnect cavity, and remove the access lid so the disconnect plug, blade set, guide rail, interlock pins, latch window, and surrounding sealing edge are all visible in one service view.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T2 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T3
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Pull the service disconnect straight out without rotating it, then inspect the male blades, female socket faces, guide ribs, CPA feature, handle pivot, and latch hook for burrs, arc marks, plastic rollover, uneven insertion witness lines, or debris in the guide channel.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T3 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T4
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Compare the suspect disconnect with a known-good reference or spare, checking seating depth, latch travel, blade exposure, guide-rib alignment, and the shoulder position of the interlock pins so the seat issue is tied to geometry rather than to feel alone.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T4 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T5
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Rework the seat condition by correcting the misaligned guide feature, removing only the minor burr or displaced plastic that blocks full travel, and cleaning the contact area without changing the original blade profile, terminal tension, or latch geometry. The correction has to preserve the blade path and the interlock-pin relationship rather than forcing the handle closed by cosmetic trimming.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T5 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T6
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Reinstall the service disconnect in one continuous motion, confirm full blade entry, verify the latch hook snaps fully into the window, and check that the orange handle now remains flush with the cover plane instead of rebounding or sitting with a visible gap.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T6 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T7
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Restore the service lid, wake the pack, and confirm through two seat checks that the HVIL path, service-disconnect status, and ready-state permissive remain stable with no handle movement, no latch relaxation, and no repeat interlock warning. The release condition is a disconnect that seats flush and keeps the HVIL path stable after the lid and surrounding trim return to final position.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T7 retained verbatim from the source document.
